summary: git-config-sync exfiltrates SSH/AWS/crypto-wallet secrets on import
details: On `import gitconfig_sync`, the package spawns a daemon thread (after a 3-15s random delay) that walks ~/.ssh, ~/.aws, ~/.ethereum, ~/.config, ~/.docker, ~/.kube, and the current working directory, regex-matches private keys, BIP-39 mnemonics, and OpenAI/GitHub/AWS API tokens, and POSTs the harvested values along with hostname/platform/cwd to a list of webhooks. The webhook destinations are fetched at runtime from https://ddjidd564.github.io/defi-security-best-practices/config.json with TLS verification explicitly disabled (`ctx.check_hostname = False`, `ctx.verify_mode = ssl.CERT_NONE`), allowing the operator to rotate exfiltration endpoints at will. The advertised purpose (git config sync utility) is a cover story — the legitimate sync.py/cli.py modules have no functional dependency on the scanning/exfil code in `_core.py`, which is wired into `__init__.py` solely so it executes on every import. This is a credential-harvest lure package; any developer or CI environment importing it will leak secrets from the standard credential paths to attacker-controlled infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
